results.question: DAU by platform last 7 days
results.answerText:
The artifact is ready — DAU split by OS platform per day, using {{field:active_daily.dau}} grouped by {{field:active_daily.os_platform}}.

**iOS dominates daily actives.** On 2026-06-17 (the last full day in the data), iOS posted ~104k DAU against ~67k for Android — roughly a 1.5× gap — plus a small ~2.4k `na` (unattributed) bucket.

One data caveat: only **17–18 Jun** are loaded right now, and **18 Jun is clearly partial** (iOS drops to ~23k, Android to ~13k), so don't read the second day as a real decline. The query is pinned to the full 17–23 Jun window, so the chart will fill in automatically as the remaining days land.

results.question: revenue by platform last 7 days
results.answerText:
Recharge revenue for **Jun 17–23, 2026** breaks down across two platforms, with {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} grouped by {{field:user_recharge_daily.os_platform}}:

- **Android** — ₫549.7M (~73%)
- **iOS** — ₫199.1M (~27%)

Android out-earns iOS by roughly 2.8×, so the bulk of mobile spend on CFM VN is coming from Android players this week. Open the card to switch to a daily trend or layer in payment channel.

results.question: ROAS by platform last 7 days
results.answerText:
The artifact is ready. ROAS is computed from {{field:game_key_metrics.rev}} ÷ {{field:game_key_metrics.cost_vnd}}, using the cube's native {{field:game_key_metrics.roas}} measure split by {{field:game_key_metrics.platform}} for 2026-06-17 → 2026-06-23.

- **iOS — 18.6×** (443.7M VND revenue on 23.8M VND spend) — by far the most efficient channel.
- **Android — 3.0×** (182.0M VND revenue on 60.7M VND spend) — profitable but heavily outspent relative to its return.

Two rows are noise: `all` carries spend (4.8M VND) with no attributed revenue, and `na` has revenue but no recorded cost (ROAS undefined). The clear story is that iOS spend is converting at ~6× the efficiency of Android — worth investigating whether more budget can shift toward iOS without saturating it.

results.question: installs by platform last 7 days
results.answerText: Over the last 7 days (2026-06-17 → 06-23), CFM VN logged **2,527 total installs**, split by {{field:game_key_metrics.platform}}: **Android 1,812 (~72%)** and **iOS 715 (~28%)** — Android outpacing iOS roughly 2.5-to-1. The `na` and `all` platform buckets returned 0, so all real installs came from the two named platforms. The card uses {{field:game_key_metrics.installs}} from the daily key-metrics mart; click through to re-slice by country, media source, or paid-vs-organic.

results.question: CPI by platform last 7 days
results.answerText:
Over the last 7 days (Jun 17–23), {{field:game_key_metrics.cpi_vnd}} is essentially flat across platforms: **~33,510 VND** on Android vs **~33,300 VND** on iOS — a difference under 1%. The volume is where they split: Android pulled **1,812 installs** on **60.7M VND** of {{field:game_key_metrics.cost_vnd}}, while iOS pulled **715 installs** on **23.8M VND**, so Android is carrying roughly 70% of paid acquisition.

Note the `all` and `na` platform rows carry some spend (4.8M VND) but **0 attributable installs**, so their CPI is null — that's unattributed/organic-bucket cost worth a separate look if you're auditing spend efficiency.

results.question: show Minnow last 7 days
results.answerText:
"Minnow" daily active users sat in a tight band of **51.7K–53.2K** from Jun 17–22, so engagement among low-spend (minnow-tier) payers is flat — no week-over-week erosion. The Jun 23 figure (11.6K) is just today's partial count, not a real decline.

Two notes on how I read your request:
- You didn't name a metric, so I defaulted to {{field:active_daily.dau}} (daily active users). Say the word if you meant revenue, paying users, playtime, etc.
- "Minnow" was treated as the {{field:mf_users.payer_tier}} = `minnow` spender tier.

results.question: Whale this month
results.answerText:
The chart tracks **daily active whale-tier players** ({{field:active_daily.dau}} filtered to {{field:mf_users.payer_tier}} = `whale`) across June 2026. Whale activity is steady — hovering between ~5,990 and ~6,170 users a day through the first ten days, with no clear up or down trend.

One note: "Whale this month" didn't name a metric, so I defaulted it to **active users (DAU)**. If you actually meant whale **revenue/recharge** or **headcount** this month, say the word and I'll swap the measure.

results.question: Whale revenue last 7 days
results.answerText: Whale-tier players brought in roughly **7.32B VND** in recharge revenue over the last 7 days (Jun 17–23). The trend is front-loaded: it ramped from 328M VND on Jun 17 to a peak of **3.18B VND on Jun 19**, then declined steadily through the week, with Jun 23 (55M VND) reflecting a partial current day rather than a real cliff. Built from {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} filtered to {{field:mf_users.payer_tier}} = `whale`. Open the artifact to change the window or break it down by {{field:user_recharge_daily.payment_channel}} or {{field:user_recharge_daily.country_code}}.

results.question: compare Dolphin month over month
results.answerText: I've surfaced the options above — pick one and I'll build the month-over-month comparison.

results.question: show WAU last 7 days
results.answerText: **311,445** unique users were active at least once over the trailing 7 days (2026-06-17 → 2026-06-23), measured by {{field:active_daily.wau}}. This is a single rolling distinct-user count — every user is counted once no matter how many of the 7 days they logged in. Open the artifact to switch to a weekly-grain trend or add a DAU breakdown.

results.question: compare WAU month over month
results.answerText: Both options stay within June since that's the full extent of the data. Tell me which framing you want, or if a different metric would be more useful.

results.question: show DAU last 7 days
results.answerText: DAU stayed flat and healthy over the window — between **172.9K and 178.5K** from June 17–22, with the peak of **178,491** on June 21 ({{field:active_daily.dau}} computed from the per-user daily snapshot cube). The June 23 value (36,408) is just today's partial day still accumulating, so ignore it as a trend signal. The artifact above opens the full daily series in the Playground.

results.question: revenue last 30 days
results.answerText: Total recharge revenue for the trailing 30-day window is **≈ 17.03B VND**, drawn from {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} bucketed by {{field:user_recharge_daily.log_date}}. Daily revenue sits around ~945M VND on average, with a clear spike to **2.96B VND on Jun 12** (and a secondary peak Jun 5). The pipeline only has rows from Jun 1 onward in this window, and Jun 18 (119M) looks like a partial/incomplete day — so treat the effective coverage as Jun 1–17 full days. Open the artifact to slice this by {{field:user_recharge_daily.payment_channel}}, {{field:user_recharge_daily.country_code}}, or VIP level.

results.question: DAU by country last 7 days
results.answerText:
Distinct daily active users over the last 7 days ({{field:active_daily.dau}}) split by player first-seen country ({{field:mf_users.country}}). The breakdown is heavily concentrated: **VN ≈ 298K**, **OT (other) ≈ 14K**, plus a tiny unattributed slice — i.e. ~95% of activity is Vietnam-origin, as expected for cfm_vn.

A note on grain: in-game `country_code` on the activity snapshot reports VN-only, so this chart uses each user's first-seen country instead to surface the small non-VN tail. The join drops a handful of users without a master-profile match, so the VN total here (~298K) is marginally below the raw all-users DAU (~311K).

results.question: top 10 players by revenue
results.answerText:
Here are the **top 10 players by revenue**, ranked by total lifetime recharge from {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}}.

The standout is the #1 player at **₫446M** — more than double the #2 spender (₫182M). Ranks #2 through #10 are tightly packed between **₫143M and ₫182M**, so apart from the single outlier at the top, the rest of the leaderboard is fairly even.

Note: I ranked by **lifetime** total spend (no date filter), since "top players by revenue" usually means all-time whales. If you'd rather see a specific window (e.g. last 7 or 30 days), just say so and I'll re-rank.
